paragraphs.en: In her new book Cogs and Monsters: What Economics Is, and What It Should Be, Diane Coyle, an economist at Cambridge University, argues that the digital economy requires new ways of thinking about progress. “Whatever we mean by the economy growing, by things getting better, the gains will have to be more evenly shared than in the recent past,” she writes.
paragraphs.zh: 剑桥大学经济学家黛安·科伊尔 (Diane Coyle) 在她的新书《齿轮与怪物：经济学是什么，以及它应该是什么》中指出，数字经济需要新的思维方式来思考进步。她写道：“无论我们所说的经济增长、情况好转是什么意思，收益的分配都必须比最近更加平均。”

paragraphs.en: “An economy of tech millionaires or billionaires and gig workers, with middle-income jobs undercut by automation, will not be politically sustainable.”
paragraphs.zh: “科技百万富翁或亿万富翁和零工工人的经济，以及中等收入工作岗位因自动化而受到削弱，在政治上是不可持续的。”

paragraphs.en: Improving living standards and increasing prosperity for more people will require greater use of digital technologies to boost productivity in various sectors, including health care and construction, says Coyle. But people can’t be expected to embrace the changes if they’re not seeing the benefits — if they’re just seeing good jobs being destroyed.
paragraphs.zh: 科伊尔表示，要提高更多人的生活水平并促进繁荣，就需要更多地利用数字技术来提高各个行业的生产力，包括医疗保健和建筑业。但如果人们没有看到好处——如果他们只是看到好工作被摧毁，就不能指望他们接受这些变化。

paragraphs.en: In a recent interview, Coyle said she fears that tech’s inequality problem could be a roadblock to deploying AI. “We’re talking about disruption,” she says. “These are transformative technologies that change the ways we spend our time every day, that change business models that succeed.” To make such “tremendous changes,” she adds, you need social buy-in.
paragraphs.zh: 在最近的一次采访中，科伊尔表示，她担心科技领域的不平等问题可能会成为部署人工智能的障碍。 “我们正在谈论颠覆，”她说。 “这些都是革命性的技术，它们改变了我们每天度过时间的方式，改变了成功的商业模式。”她补充道，要做出如此“巨大的改变”，你需要社会的支持。

paragraphs.en: Instead, says Coyle, resentment is simmering among many as the benefits are perceived to go to elites in a handful of prosperous cities.
paragraphs.zh: 科伊尔表示，相反，许多人的不满情绪正在酝酿之中，因为人们认为这些好处惠及了少数繁荣城市的精英。

paragraphs.en: According to the Brookings Institution, a short list of eight American cities that included San Francisco, San Jose, Boston, and Seattle had roughly 38% of all tech jobs by 2019. New AI technologies are particularly concentrated: Brookings’s Mark Muro and Sifan Liu estimate that just 15 cities account for two-thirds of the AI assets and capabilities in the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 根据布鲁金斯学会的数据，到 2019 年，旧金山、圣何塞、波士顿和西雅图等 8 个美国城市占据了大约 38% 的科技岗位。新的人工智能技术尤其集中：布鲁金斯学会的 Mark Muro 和 Sifan Liu 估计，仅 15 个城市就拥有美国人工智能资产和能力的三分之二。

paragraphs.en: The dominance of a few cities in the invention and commercialization of AI means that geographical disparities in wealth will continue to soar. Not only will this foster political and social unrest, but it could, as Coyle suggests, hold back the sorts of AI technologies needed for regional economies to grow.
paragraphs.zh: 少数城市在人工智能的发明和商业化方面占据主导地位，这意味着财富的地域差距将继续扩大。这不仅会加剧政治和社会动荡，而且正如科伊尔所说，它可能会阻碍区域经济增长所需的人工智能技术。

paragraphs.en: Part of the solution could lie in somehow loosening the stranglehold that Big Tech has on defining the AI agenda. That will likely take increased federal funding for research independent of the tech giants.
paragraphs.zh: 部分解决方案可能在于以某种方式放松大型科技公司对定义人工智能议程的束缚。这可能需要增加联邦资金用于独立于科技巨头的研究。

paragraphs.en: A more immediate response is to broaden our digital imaginations to conceive of AI technologies that don’t simply replace jobs but expand opportunities in the sectors that different parts of the country care most about, like health care, education, and manufacturing.
paragraphs.zh: 更直接的反应是扩大我们的数字想象力，构想人工智能技术，这些技术不仅会取代工作岗位，而且会扩大该国不同地区最关心的行业的机会，例如医疗保健、教育和制造业。

questions.text: Coyle argues in her new book that economic growth should ______
According to Paragraph 2, digital technologies should be used to ______
What does Coyle fear about transformative technologies? ________
options: They may be impractical to deploy.
They may be unwelcome to the public.

questions.text: Several American cities are mentioned to show ______
options: the uneven distribution of AI technologies in the US.
the disappointing prospect of tech jobs in the US.
the fast progress of US regional economies.
the increasing significance of US AI assets.

questions.text: With regard to Coyle’s concern, the author suggests ______
options: raising funds to start new AI projects.
guarding against the side effects of AI.
redefining the role of AI technologies.

paragraphs.en: The UK is facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood, Confor has warned. The forestry and wood trade body has called for urgent action to reduce the country’s reliance on timber imports and provide a stable supply of wood for future generations. Currently only 20 percent of the UK’s wood requirement is home-grown while it remains the second-largest net importer of timber in the world.
paragraphs.zh: Confor警告说，由于无法种植树木来生产木材，英国未来将面临建筑危机。林业和木材贸易机构呼吁采取紧急行动，减少该国对木材进口的依赖，并为子孙后代提供稳定的木材供应。目前，英国只有 20% 的木材需求是本土生产的，但英国仍然是世界第二大木材净进口国。

paragraphs.en: Coming at a time of fresh incentives from the UK government for landowners to grow more trees, the trade body says these don’t go far enough and fail to promote the benefits of planting them to boost timber supplies. “Not only are we facing a carbon crisis now, but we will also be facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood,” said Stuart Goodall, chief executive of Confor. “For decades we have not taken responsibility for investing in our domestic wood supply, leaving us exposed to fluctuating prices and fighting for future supplies of wood as global demand rises and our own supplies fall.”
paragraphs.zh: 该贸易机构表示，在英国政府出台新的激励措施鼓励土地所有者种植更多树木之际，这些措施还不够，也未能宣传种植树木以增加木材供应的好处。 Confor 首席执行官斯图尔特·古道尔 (Stuart Goodall) 表示：“我们现在不仅面临碳危机，未来还将面临建筑危机，因为无法种植树木来生产木材。” “几十年来，我们没有承担起投资国内木材供应的责任，这使我们面临价格波动的风险，并且随着全球需求的上升和我们自己的供应下降，我们不得不为未来的木材供应而战。”

paragraphs.en: The UK has ideal conditions for growing wood to build low-carbon homes and is a global leader in certifying that its forests are sustainably managed, Confor says. While around three quarters of Scottish homes are built from Scottish timber, the use of home-grown wood in England is only around 25 percent. The causes of the UK’s current position are complex and range from outdated perceptions of productive forestry to the decimation of trees by grey squirrels. It also encompasses significant hesitation on behalf of farmers and other landowners to invest in long-term planting projects.
paragraphs.zh: Confor表示，英国拥有种植木材建造低碳住宅的理想条件，并且在证明其森林得到可持续管理方面处于全球领先地位。虽然大约四分之三的苏格兰房屋是用苏格兰木材建造的，但英格兰本土木材的使用率只有 25% 左右。英国目前处境的原因很复杂，从对生产性林业的过时观念到灰松鼠对树木的大量砍伐。它还包括农民和其他土地所有者对投资长期种植项目的严重犹豫。

paragraphs.en: While productive tree planting can deliver real financial benefits to rural economies and contribute to the UK’s net-zero strategy, the focus of government support continues to be on food production and the rewilding and planting of native woodland solely for biodiversity.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然高效植树可以为农村经济带来真正的经济利益，并有助于英国的净零排放战略，但政府支持的重点仍然是粮食生产以及纯粹为了生物多样性而重新野化和种植原生林地。

paragraphs.en: Goodall added: “While food production and biodiversity health are clearly of critical importance, we need our land to also provide secure supplies of wood for construction, manufacturing and contribute to net-zero.”
paragraphs.zh: 古道尔补充道：“虽然粮食生产和生物多样性健康显然至关重要，但我们还需要我们的土地为建筑、制造提供安全的木材供应，并为净零排放做出贡献。”

paragraphs.en: “While the UK government has stated its ambition for more tree planting, there has been little action on the ground. Confor is now calling for much greater impetus behind those aspirations to ensure we have enough wood to meet increasing demand.”
paragraphs.zh: “虽然英国政府表达了更多植树的雄心，但实际行动却很少。Confor 现在呼吁为这些愿望提供更大的推动力，以确保我们有足够的木材来满足不断增长的需求。”

questions.text: It can be learned from Paragraph 1 that the UK needs to ______
options: lift its control on timber imports.

questions.text: According to Confor, the UK government’s fresh incentives ______
options: can hardly address a construction crisis.
are believed to come at a wrong time.
seem to be misleading for landowners.
will be too costly to put into practice.

questions.text: The UK’s exposure to fluctuating wood prices is a result of ______
options: the governments inaction on timber imports.
the competition among timber traders at home.
wood producers’ motive to maximise profits.

questions.text: Which of the following causes the shortage of wood supply in the UK? ________
options: Outdated technologies of the wood industry.

questions.text: What does Goodall think the UK government should do? ________
options: Subsidise the building of low-carbon homes.
Pay greater attention to boosting rural economies.
Provide more support for productive tree planting.
Give priority to pursuing its net-zero strategy.

paragraphs.en: One of the biggest challenges in keeping unsafe aging drivers off the road is convincing them that it’s time to turn over the keys. “It’s a complete life-changer” when someone stops — or is forced to stop — driving, said former risk manager Anne M. Menke.
paragraphs.zh: 让不安全的老年司机远离道路的最大挑战之一是说服他们是时候交出钥匙了。前风险经理 Anne M. Menke 表示，当有人停止或被迫停止驾驶时，“这将彻底改变生活”。

paragraphs.en: “The American Medical Association advises physicians that in situations where clear evidence of substantial driving impairment implies a strong threat to patient and public safety, and where the physician’s advice to discontinue driving privileges is ignored, it is desirable and ethical to notify the Department of Motor Vehicles,” Menke wrote. “Some states require physicians to report, others allow but do not mandate reports, while a few consider a report a breach of confidentiality. There could be liability and penalties if a physician does not act in accordance with state laws on reporting and confidentiality,” she counseled.
paragraphs.zh: 门克写道：“美国医学会建议医生，如果有明显证据表明严重驾驶障碍意味着对患者和公共安全构成严重威胁，并且医生停止驾驶特权的建议被忽视，则最好通知机动车辆部，这也是合乎道德的。” “一些州要求医生报告，其他州允许但不强制报告，而少数州则认为报告违反保密规定。如果医生不按照州有关报告和保密的法律行事，可能会承担责任和处罚，”她建议道。

paragraphs.en: Part of the problem in keeping older drivers safe is that the difficulties are addressed piecemeal by different professions with different focuses, including gerontologists, highway administration officials, automotive engineers and others, said gerontologist Elizabeth Dugan.
paragraphs.zh: 老年学家伊丽莎白·杜根 (Elizabeth Dugan) 表示，保障老年驾驶员安全的部分问题在于，不同专业人士以不同的侧重点零碎地解决了这些困难，其中包括老年学家、高速公路管理官员、汽车工程师等。

paragraphs.en: “There’s not a National Institute of Older Driver Studies,” she said. “We need better evidence on what makes drivers unsafe” and what can help, said Dugan.
paragraphs.zh: “没有国家老年驾驶员研究所，”她说。杜根说，“我们需要更好的证据来证明是什么导致司机不安全”以及什么可以提供帮助。

paragraphs.en: One thing that does seem to work is requiring drivers to report in person for license renewal. Mandatory in-person renewal was associated with a 31 percent reduction in fatal crashes involving drivers 85 or older, according to one study. Passing vision tests also produced a similar decline in fatal crashes for those drivers, although there appeared to be no benefit from combining the two.
paragraphs.zh: 似乎确实有效的一件事是要求司机亲自报告以更新驾照。一项研究表明，强制当面续保可以使 85 岁或以上驾驶员的致命车祸减少 31%。通过视力测试也使这些司机的致命车祸发生率类似地下降，尽管将两者结合起来似乎没有任何好处。

paragraphs.en: Many older drivers don’t see eye doctors or can’t afford to. Primary care providers have their hands full and may not be able to follow through with patients who have trouble driving because they can’t turn their heads or remember where they are going — or have gotten shorter and haven’t changed their seat settings sufficiently to reach car pedals easily.
paragraphs.zh: 许多老年司机不去看眼科医生，或者负担不起。初级保健提供者忙得不可开交，可能无法跟进那些驾驶困难的患者，因为他们无法转动头部或记住自己要去哪里，或者变得更矮并且没有充分改变座椅设置以轻松到达汽车踏板。

paragraphs.en: As long as there are other cars on the roads, self-driving cars won’t solve the problems of crashes, said Dugan. Avoiding dangers posed by all those human drivers would require too many algorithms, she said. But we need to do more to improve safety, said Dugan. “If we’re going to have 100-year lives, we need cars that a 90-year-old can drive comfortably.”
paragraphs.zh: 杜根说，只要道路上还有其他汽车，自​​动驾驶汽车就无法解决碰撞问题。她说，避免所有人类司机造成的危险需要太多的算法。但杜根说，我们需要采取更多措施来提高安全性。 “如果我们想要活到 100 岁，我们就需要 90 岁的老人也能舒适地驾驶的汽车。”

questions.text: According to Paragraph 1, keeping unsafe aging drivers off the road ______
options: can be a tough task to complete.
will be beneficial to their health.

questions.text: The American Medical Association’s advice ______
According to Dugan, efforts to keep older drivers safe ______
Some older drivers have trouble driving because they tend to ______
Dugan thinks that the solution to the problems of crashes may lie in ______
options: adjusting the age limit for drivers.

paragraphs.en: If you look at the apps on your phone, chances are you have at least one related to your health — and probably several. Whether it is a mental health app, a fitness tracker, a connected health device or something else, many of us are taking advantage of this technology to keep better track of our health in some shape or form. Recent research from the Organization for the Review of Care and Health Applications found that 350,000 health apps were available on the market, 90,000 of which launched in 2020 alone.
paragraphs.zh: 如果您查看手机上的应用程序，您可能至少有一个与您的健康相关的应用程序 - 甚至可能有多个。无论是心理健康应用程序、健身追踪器、联网健康设备还是其他产品，我们中的许多人都在利用这项技术以某种形式更好地跟踪我们的健康状况。护理和健康应用审查组织最近的研究发现，市场上有 350,000 个健康应用程序，其中 90,000 个是在 2020 年推出的。

paragraphs.en: While these apps have a great deal to offer, it is not always clear how the personal information we input is collected, safeguarded and shared online. Existing health privacy law, such as the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, is primarily focused on the way hospitals, doctors’ offices, clinics and insurance companies store health records online.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然这些应用程序可以提供很多功能，但我们并不总是清楚如何收集、保护和在线共享我们输入的个人信息。现有的健康隐私法，例如《健康保险流通和责任法案》，主要关注医院、医生办公室、诊所和保险公司在线存储健康记录的方式。

paragraphs.en: The health information these apps and health data tracking wearables are collecting typically do not receive the same legal protections.
paragraphs.zh: 这些应用程序和跟踪可穿戴设备收集的健康信息通常不会受到相同的法律保护。

paragraphs.en: Without additional protections in place, companies may share (and potentially monetize) personal health information in a way consumers may not have authorized or anticipated. In 2021, Flo Health faced a Federal Trade Commission (FTC) investigation. The FTC alleged in a complaint that “despite express privacy claims, the company took control of users’ sensitive fertility data and shared it with third parties.” Flo Health and the FTC settled the matter with a Consent Order requiring the company to get app users’ express affirmative consent before sharing their health information as well as to instruct the third parties to delete the data they had obtained.
paragraphs.zh: 如果没有额外的保护措施，公司可能会以消费者未授权或预期的方式共享（并可能将其货币化）个人健康信息。 2021 年，Flo Health 面临联邦贸易委员会 (FTC) 调查。美国联邦贸易委员会在一份投诉中称，“尽管有明确的隐私声明，该公司还是控制了用户的敏感生育数据，并将其与第三方共享。” Flo Health 和联邦贸易委员会通过一项同意令解决了此事，要求该公司在分享用户的健康信息之前获得应用程序用户的明确同意，并指示第三方删除他们获得的数据。

paragraphs.en: Section 5 of the FTC Act empowers the FTC to initiate enforcement action against unfair or deceptive acts, meaning the FTC can only act after the fact if a company’s privacy practices are misleading or cause unjustified consumer harm. While the FTC is doing what it can to ensure apps are keeping their promises to consumers around the handling of their sensitive health information, the rate at which these health apps are hitting the market demonstrates just how immense of a challenge this is.
paragraphs.zh: 《联邦贸易委员会法案》第 5 条授权联邦贸易委员会针对不公平或欺骗行为发起执法行动，这意味着联邦贸易委员会只能在公司的隐私做法具有误导性或造成不合理的消费者伤害的情况下采取事后行动。虽然联邦贸易委员会正在尽其所能确保应用程序在处理敏感健康信息方面履行对消费者的承诺，但这些健康应用程序进入市场的速度表明这是一个多么巨大的挑战。

paragraphs.en: As to the prospects for federal legislation, commentators suggest that comprehensive federal privacy legislation seems unlikely in the short term. States have begun implementing their own solutions to shore up protections for consumer-generated health data. California has been at the forefront of state privacy efforts with the California Consumer Privacy Act of 2018. Virginia, Colorado and Utah have also recently passed state consumer data privacy legislation.
paragraphs.zh: 至于联邦立法的前景，评论人士表示，短期内似乎不太可能出台全面的联邦隐私立法。各州已开始实施自己的解决方案，以加强对消费者生成的健康数据的保护。通过《2018 年加州消费者隐私法案》，加州一直走在州隐私保护工作的最前沿。弗吉尼亚州、科罗拉多州和犹他州最近也通过了州消费者数据隐私立法。

questions.text: The research findings are cited in Paragraph 1 to show ______
What does the author imply about existing health privacy law? ________
options: Its coverage needs to be extended.

questions.text: Before sharing its users’ health information, Flo Health is required to ______
options: seek the approval of the FTC.

questions.text: What challenge is the FTC currently faced with? ________
options: The rapid increase in new health apps.
The subtle deceptiveness of health apps.
The difficulty in assessing consumer harm.

questions.text: It can be learned from the last paragraph that health data protection ______
options: has been embraced by health app developers.
has been a focus of federal policy-making.
has gained legislative support in some states.
